title: Loa Typed Model Error (cycle-102 Sprint 1, AC-1.1)
description: Anti-silent-degradation typed-error envelope. Every model failure surface (cheval, model-adapter shim, fallback walk, audit-emit payload) MUST conform. The 10 error_class values cover the cheval exception taxonomy (SDD section 4.1) plus probe-gate outcomes (LOCAL_NETWORK_FAILURE) and chain-exhaustion verdicts (FALLBACK_EXHAUSTED). UNKNOWN is the explicit escape hatch — it MUST populate original_exception with the full stacktrace so triage can reclassify into a typed class in a follow-up cycle.

error_class.description: Closed enumeration of error categories. UNKNOWN is the only acceptable bucket for unmapped failures and MUST be paired with original_exception.

severity.description: Operator-visible severity. Maps to the exit-code table at SDD section 4.1: WARN -> exit 0 (advisory, fallback succeeded or probe-itself flake); ERROR -> reserved for future use (currently no scenario emits ERROR); BLOCKER -> chain exhaustion or unrecoverable config error -> non-zero exit.

message_redacted.description: Human-readable error message AFTER lib/log-redactor.{sh,py} scrubs API keys, tokens, file paths, emails, IPs, JWTs, etc. NFR-Sec-1. Cap chosen to bound audit payload size while preserving enough context for triage.

provider.description: Provider key (matches model-config.yaml providers map). Examples: openai, anthropic, google, bedrock.

model.description: Model key (matches model-config.yaml models map). Examples: gpt-5.5-pro, claude-opus-4-7, gemini-3.1-pro.

retryable.description: Whether the failure is transient. RateLimitError -> true; ConfigError -> false. Callers MAY use this hint to decide whether to retry the same model vs. step the fallback chain.

fallback_from.description: Populated when this error caused a fallback. The model the call started on.

fallback_to.description: Populated when this error caused a fallback. The model the call fell back to. Equal to the empty string is forbidden — use FALLBACK_EXHAUSTED with no fallback_to instead.

original_exception.description: REQUIRED when error_class=UNKNOWN. Stacktrace + exception message from cheval/Python/etc. Used by triage cycles to reclassify into a typed class. SHOULD NOT be populated for any other error_class — typed classes already encode the failure mode. NFR-Sec-1 emitter contract (cycle-102 Sprint 1B T1B.1, HIGH security; closes BB iter-5 FIND-005): emitters MUST run this string through lib/log-redactor.{sh,py} BEFORE populating. The audit chain is hash-chain immutable per cycle-098; any token, key, prompt fragment, or PII that lands in this field is PERMANENT and unrecoverable. The 16384-byte cap (2x message_redacted's cap) bounds audit-payload size; the schema enforces only shape (string + maxLength). Semantic redaction is non-negotiable emitter responsibility — there is NO downstream lint that retroactively scrubs the chain. Sprint 1B T1.7 (audit_emit wiring) MUST land emitter-side redaction before any MODELINV writer ships; tests/unit/model-error-schema.bats X1 (T1B.1 contract pin) asserts a fake bearer-token shape is redacted before validation. Closes BB iter-4 FIND-003 (med, original_exception cap rationale) AND BB iter-5 FIND-005 (Anthropic single-model, re-classified HIGH).

ts_utc.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the failure. Audit envelope sets this; emitters MAY pre-populate.
